reasoning: The ensures clause `usize2page_entry_perm(ret) =~= pe.perm` already covers all fields including user. The individual field ensures are redundant convenience; the "asymmetry" of omitting one is a style issue, not a spec gap. The property trivially follows from the requires.

This is exactly how x86-64 page table entries work by hardware design. Bit 63 is the NX/XD bit and bits 51:12 are the physical address — they occupy non-overlapping bit positions. MEM_MASK correctly extracts bits 47:12 for the address while PAGE_ENTRY_EXECUTE_MASK extracts bit 63. There is no overlap; the spec faithfully models the hardware encoding.
